title: Land Transport Regulatory Authority (Registration and Certification of Crew) Regulations, 2020
title_en: Land Transport Regulatory Authority (Registration and Certification of Crew) Regulations, 2020

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Land Transport Regulatory Authority (Registration and Certification of Crew) Regulations, 2020.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These Regulations shall apply to crew of regulated sectors as construed under these Regulations.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires— “ Act ” means the Land Transport Regulatory Authority Act; [Cap. 413] “ Authority ” means the Land Transport Regulatory Authority established under section 4 of the Act ; [Cap. 413] “ Certificate of competence ” means a permit issued by the Authority to a train driver of a regulated sector. “ certificate ” means a permit granted to a train driver ; “ Certification Committee ” means a committee responsible for certification of train drivers established under regulation 7 ; “ crew ” means a train driver , train guard, train conductor and coach attendant; “ demerit point system ” means a system of penalizing drivers of train for violating provisions of train working Rules by assigning points for each type of violation and revoking or suspending the driver’s certificate if a certain number of points are accrued; “ Director General ” means the Director General of the Authority appointed under section 15 of the Act ; [Cap.413] “ medical practitioner ” means a medical practitioner registered with the Medical Council of Tanganyika; “ narcotic substance ” has the meaning assigned to that expression under the Drugs and Prevention of Illicit Traffic in Drugs Act ; [Cap.95] “ officer of the Authority ” means director, manager or any other officer authorised by the Authority ; “ passenger ” means any person who is traveling in a train , who possesses a valid train ticket and includes a child of apparent age of not more than five years travelling in a train ; “ time table ” means a document issued by the railways authority prescribing time of departure and arrival of train at different stations in a specified route; “ train driver ” a certified person who operates a train ; and “ train ” includes a locomotive, a shunting locomotive, rail car, motor trolley when given operating number and permit to occupy a railway section. Part II – Certification of train drivers

provisions.title: Certification of drivers
provisions.content: (1) A person shall not drive a train without being certified by the Authority . (2) A driver of cross border train to Mainland Tanzania shall ensure that he possesses— (a) a certificate of competence issued by the Authority in accordance with these Regulations; or (b) a certificate of competence issued by a relevant authority from the respective country. Part III – Procedure for obtaining certificate

provisions.title: Application for certificate
provisions.content: (1) A person who intends to be certified by the Authority as a train driver shall submit an application to the Authority in a form prescribed in the First Schedule. (2) The application under sub-regulation (1) , shall be accompanied with: (a) a certified copy of birth certificate or any relevant document for proof of birth; (b) a certified copy of Certificate of Secondary Education Examination (CSEE) or its equivalent; (c) a certified copy of a basic training in locomotive driving from a recognized railway institute; (d) proof of completion of at least two years practical and internship training in railway operations; (e) proof of payment of certification fees; and (f) a recent medical examination report from a medical practitioner . (3) Notwithstanding sub-regulation (1) a train driver who— (a) has completed basic training in locomotive driving; and (b) possesses experience of not less than five years in locomotive driving, shall submit an application to the Authority as may be prescribed by the Authority .

provisions.title: Issuance of certificate
provisions.content: The Authority shall, where it is satisfied that the application requirements under these Regulations are complied with, issue a certificate bearing the following— (a) name and address of the certified train driver ; (b) duration of the certificate ; and (c) conditions of the certificate .

provisions.title: Certification Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Director General shall establish a Certification Committee which shall consist of three members as follows— (a) a certified railway assessor; (b) a certified locomotive inspector; and (c) a qualified psychologist or a medical doctor. (2) The tenure of the members of the Committee shall be three years and may be renewed once. (3) The Authority shall prepare certification guidelines that will enable the committee perform its functions under these Regulations.

provisions.title: Functions of Certification Committee
provisions.content: The functions of the Certification Committee shall be as follows: (a) determination of applications for certification of train drivers; (b) preparation of assessment report and submit to the Director General ; and (c) performing any other function relating to certification of Train drivers as may be directed by the Director General .

provisions.title: Issuance of certificate of competence
provisions.content: (1) The Authority shall, where it is satisfied that the application requirements under these Regulations are complied with, issue a certificate of competence. (2) The certificate of competence issued under this regulation shall: (a) be in a form prescribed in the Second Schedule; and (b) contain one hundred (100) merit points as prescribed in the Fifth Schedule. (3) A certificate of competence issued under this regulation shall be valid for a period of two years.

provisions.title: Renewal of certificate
provisions.content: (1) A driver who intends to renew a certificate of competence may apply to the Authority within sixty days prior to the expiry date. (2) The application for renewal of a certificate shall be accompanied with: (a) proof of payment of certification fees; and (b) a recent medical examination report from a medical practitioner .

provisions.title: Certification fees
provisions.content: An applicant for a certificate shall pay to the Authority fees as set out in the Third Schedule.

provisions.title: Transit permit
provisions.content: (1) Any driver who intends to cross border to Tanzania shall submit online application in a prescribed form under the Fourth Schedule. (2) Notwithstanding regulation 5 , the Authority may issue a transit permit to cross border train in accordance with bilateral or regional agreement.

provisions.title: Demerit points
provisions.content: (1) A driver of a train shall be subjected to a demerit point system set out in the Fifth Schedule. (2) The Authority shall record demerit points accumulated from the train driver ’s misconduct for the period of two years. (3) The points to be deducted from the certificate of competence shall be as prescribed in the Fifth Schedule.

provisions.title: Suspension or cancellation of certificate of competence
provisions.content: (1) Where a train driver contravenes these Regulations the Authority may suspend or cancel his certificate of competence in a manner prescribed in the Fifth Schedule. (2) Notwithstanding sub regulation (1) , the Authority shall, issue to the train driver a notice of seven days to show cause why the certificate shall not be suspended or cancelled. (3) Where the Authority is not satisfied with train driver ’s explanations, it shall suspend or cancel the certificate of competence of the train driver . (4) A train driver whose certificate of competence is suspended or cancelled shall— (a) in the case of a suspended certificate , not drive any train for a prescribed period of suspension; and (b) in the case of a cancelled certificate , cease to operate any train for the period of one year from the date of cancellation, and shall, in addition, immediately surrender the certificate to the Authority . (5) A person who is aggrieved by the decision of the Authority may, within fourteen days of such decision apply for review in accordance with the Act .

provisions.title: Renewal of certificate of competence
provisions.content: (1) A train driver who intends to renew a certificate of competence may, within sixty days prior to the expiry date, apply to the Authority . (2) The application for renewal of a certificate shall be accompanied by— (a) a copy of the expiring certificate of competent of the applicant; (b) proof of payment of certification fees as prescribed in the Third Schedule; and (c) a recent medical examination report from a qualified medical practitioner .

provisions.title: Certificate of competence not transferable
provisions.content: The certificate of competence issued under these Regulations shall not be transferred to any person. Part IV – Registration of train crew

provisions.title: Application for registration
provisions.content: A person who intends to work as crew in a train shall apply for registration to the Authority in the form prescribed in the Sixth Schedule.

provisions.title: Requirements for registration
provisions.content: An application for registration as crew shall be accompanied with— (a) a letter from the employer; (b) a recent medical examination report from the medical practitioner ; and (c) a certified copy of a certificate of attendance in a relevant field.

provisions.title: Issuance of registration card
provisions.content: Where the Authority — (a) is satisfied that the provisions of regulation18 have been complied with, the Authority shall issue a registration card in the manner set out in the Seventh Schedule; or (b) refuses to grant an application for registration, the Authority shall notify the applicant.

provisions.title: Transfer of registration card
provisions.content: The registration card issued under these Regulations shall not be transferred to any person.

provisions.title: Loss of registration card
provisions.content: Where a registration card issued under these Regulations is lost, mutilated or destroyed, the Authority may, issue a duplicate of the certificate . Part V – Terms and conditions for train crew

provisions.title: Terms and conditions for train driver and train guard
provisions.content: A train driver and train guard shall— (a) adhere to specified speed limits; (b) ensure that all doors are closed before moving the passenger train ; and (c) maintain a time table .

provisions.title: Prohibited conduct
provisions.content: A crew shall, while on duty shall— (a) not carry passengers in excess of the maximum number stated in the coach capacity; (b) maintain a log book and time table ; (c) wear employer’s identity card; (d) wear employer’s uniform; (e) not work under the influence of alcohol or any other narcotic substance ; (f) obey lawful order of the officer of the Authority ; and (g) not use hostile and abusive language to passengers.

provisions.title: Offences and penalties
provisions.content: (1) A train guard, train conductor and coach attendant who contravenes or fails to comply with these Regulations commits an offence and shall, on conviction, be liable to a fine of not less than fifty thousand but not exceeding one hundred thousand shillings or to imprisonment of not less than one month but not exceeding one year, or to both, fine and imprisonment. (2) A driver who contravenes or fails to comply with these Regulations commits an offence and shall, on conviction, be liable to a fine of not less than fifty thousand but not exceeding two hundred thousand shillings and will be subjected to demerit points system set out in the Fifth Schedule; or imprisonment of not less than three months but not exceeding one year, or to both, fine and imprisonment. First Schedule (Made under regulation 5(1)) Train driving certificate application form [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Second Schedule (Made under regulation 9 (2) (a)) Certificate of competence [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Third Schedule (Made under regulation 11) Application fee S/n Particular Amount(Tshs) 1 Application fee 60,000/- 2 Replacement fee 80, 000/- Note: These fee items are paid per the term of the certification Fourth Schedule (Made under regulation 12(1)) Transit permit [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Fifth Schedule (Made under regulation 9(2) (b), 13(1)(3) and 14(1)) Demerit points 1. In these Regulations, “Accumulated demerit points” means the total demerit points in a driver’s record acquired as a result of offences committed within any period of two years. 2. The authority shall take the following decision in relation to accumulated demerit points: (a) suspend for the period of three months Driver’s Certificate of competence of the driver who’s accumulated demerit points are between forty and fifty points; (b) suspend for the period of six months Driver’s Certificate of competence of the driver who’s accumulated demerit points are between thirty and forty; (c) revoke Driver’s Certificate of competence of the driver whose accumulated demerit points are below thirty; and (d) any driver whose Certificate of competence has been revoked, shall not be allowed to apply for the same certificate within the period of two years. 3. A table indicating type of offences and number of points attached to each offenceis here under: S/n Nature of offence Number of Demerit point 1 Driving under the influence of alcohol or any other narcotic substances exceeding set limit. 20 2 Driving above sectional running speed 15 3 Failure to observe and obey safety signs and signals 15 4 Driving the train while passenger’s door is open 10 5 Improper filling of the logbook 10 6 Driving a train without a line clear ticket 10 7 Blocking or obstructing intentionally other traffic at level Crossings 10 8 Driving a train without carrying safety protective equipment 5 9 Driving a train without carrying a certificate of competence 5 Total Demerit Points 100 Sixth Schedule (Made under regulation 17) Application form for train crew registration [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. Please refer to the publication document.] Seventh Schedule (Made under regulation 19) Crew registration card [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced. the Land Transport Regulatory Authority established under section 4 of the Act
a permit issued by the Authority to a train driver of a regulated sector. “ certificate ” means a permit granted to a train driver
a committee responsible for certification of train drivers established under regulation 7
a train driver , train guard, train conductor and coach attendant

a system of penalizing drivers of train for violating provisions of train working Rules by assigning points for each type of violation and revoking or suspending the driver’s certificate if a certain number of points are accrued
the Director General of the Authority appointed under section 15 of the Act
a medical practitioner registered with the Medical Council of Tanganyika
assigned to that expression under the Drugs and Prevention of Illicit Traffic in Drugs Act
director, manager or any other officer authorised by the Authority
any person who is traveling in a train , who possesses a valid train ticket and includes a child of apparent age of not more than five years travelling in a train
a document issued by the railways authority prescribing time of departure and arrival of train at different stations in a specified route
a locomotive, a shunting locomotive, rail car, motor trolley when given operating number and permit to occupy a railway section. Part II – Certification of train drivers
